response:
To compute the **sample standard deviation** of the data set:

$$
\{25, 10, 0, 9, -1, -14, -21\}
$$

---

### Step 1: Compute the mean (average) of the data set

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{25 + 10 + 0 + 9 + (-1) + (-14) + (-21)}{7}
$$

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{25 + 10 + 0 + 9 - 1 - 14 - 21}{7} = \frac{25 + 10 + 9 - 1 - 14 - 21}{7}
$$

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{34 - 36}{7} = \frac{-2}{7} \approx -0.2857
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute the squared deviations from the mean

We calculate the squared difference between each value and the mean, then sum them up.

$$
\begin{align*}
(25 - (-0.2857))^2 &= (25.2857)^2 \approx 639.21 \\
(10 - (-0.2857))^2 &= (10.2857)^2 \approx 105.78 \\
(0 - (-0.2857))^2 &= (0.2857)^2 \approx 0.0816 \\
(9 - (-0.2857))^2 &= (9.2857)^2 \approx 86.23 \\
(-1 - (-0.2857))^2 &= (-0.7143)^2 \approx 0.5103 \\
(-14 - (-0.2857))^2 &= (-13.7143)^2 \approx 188.02 \\
(-21 - (-0.2857))^2 &= (-20.7143)^2 \approx 428.94 \\
\end{align*}
$$
